fix: The service you're connecting to is not running. Start it with `npm start` or `docker-compose up`.
Missing Python package. Run `pip install <package-name>`. Check requirements.txt.
JavaScript null reference. Add a null check: `if (obj && obj.prop)` before accessing.
Cross-origin request blocked. Add CORS headers on your server or use a proxy in development.
Oracle DB invalid credentials. Check username/password in your connection string.
SSL verification failed. Check certificate expiry. Use `--insecure` only in dev, not production.
Increase heap size: `node --max-old-space-size=4096` or add more RAM to the container.
File permissions issue. Run `chmod +x <file>` or check if you need sudo.
Another process is using this port. Find it with `lsof -i :<port>` and kill it.
Endpoint doesn't exist. Check the URL, method (GET/POST), and API version.
